rationale.D01: Real regression: deploy pg-2026.04.08-c at 14:30 leaks connections; error rate 27% and climbing, NEVER auto-resolved, pool-exhausted pattern at 22x baseline. This is the one bad deploy in the storm. SEV1 must page.
rationale.D02: Deploy-correlated transient on fe-2026.04.08-a rollout; self-healed in 44s (pat-rollout-503). Expected churn during the deploy window.

rationale.D23: Chronic disk flapper, 14 fires/hr, clears on rotation in 19s. Not deploy related, still pure noise.
rationale.D24: Marginal CPU blip on the agent, auto-resolved in 36s. Noise.
rationale.D25: Same service/deploy as D01 but a cold-cache latency blip that self-healed in 49s BEFORE the pool leak manifested. Distinct from the real error-rate regression; do not double-page.

notes: Busy deploy window: 23 deploy-correlated transients + 2 flappers that self-heal, plus a same-service look-alike (D25) that recovered. Only D01 (payment-gateway pool leak from pg-2026.04.08-c) never self-heals and must page. The trap is over-suppressing D01 as 'just another deploy blip'.
